content.problemStatement:
A group of NNN people (numbered 111 through NNN) claimed that Chef is only good at cooking, but nothing else. Chef wanted to prove them wrong, so he accepted a challenge from them to guess their ages.

The NNN people gave Chef MMM pieces of information to help him. Each piece of information is described by four integers ttt, uuu, vvv and ccc with the following meaning:


If t=1t = 1t=1, the uuu-th person is older than the vvv-th person by at least ccc years.
If t=2t = 2t=2, the uuu-th person is **not** older than the vvv-th person by at least ccc years.
Chef also knows that all the ages are integers (strangely, not necessarily positive integers) and that the age of person 111 is zero.

Before Chef started thinking about the ages, he asked you to tell him whether it is even possible to determine the ages of all NNN people uniquely using the information he has. Find out if it is possible and what the ages of these NNN people are in that case.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 50,00050,00050,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
Each of the following MMM lines contains four space-separated integers ttt, uuu, vvv and ccc describing one piece of information.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to uniquely determine the ages of all the people, print a single line containing the string `"NO"` (without quotes).
Otherwise, print two lines.
The first of these lines should contain a single string `"YES"` (without quotes).
The second line should contain NNN space-separated integers denoting the ages of people 1,2,…,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,…,N respectively.
